text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet village nestled between rolling hills, there lived a girl named Ella.

Ella loved the stars. She'd sneak out into the night often to watch them dancing across the dark sky. Her favorite part was the way they seemed to know her name before she even thought to call out.

One night, she peeked at a particularly bright star and whispered, "Goodnight, bright star."

To her astonishment, the star twinkled back at her twice as brightly.

Curious, Ella decided to explore this peculiar ability. One moonlit night, while she was wandering through the quiet woods, she stumbled upon a clearing with an ancient oak tree at its center. At its feet were the most mesmerizing flowers she'd ever seen, glowing softly.

As she sat there, breathing deeply to quiet her racing heart, those glowing flowers began to sing to her. The song was like no bird song Ella had ever heard. It was soft and soothing, reminding her of
